LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARYLAND CASUALTY COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, THE COURT ANNOUNCED ITS TENTATIVE RULING FROM THE BENCH. THE MATTER WAS THEN ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE MOVING PARTY DID NOT SHIFT BURDEN. THE EVIDENCE SIMPLY DID NOT SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT PRODUCE EVIDENCE TO UNDERLYING ACTION. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 391. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY. MINI MINUTES:
APPEAR: David W. Yount, Esq. of SINNOTT, DITO et al. 352-6200 for defendant MARYLAND CASUALTY COMPANY and moving for summary adjudication
COURTCALL APPEAR: Andrew Harris, Esq. of ARONOWITZ & SKIDMORE 530/823-9736 for Plaintiff and opposing party
The Court announced its tentative ruling from the bench. The matter was then argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows -- deny motion for summary adjudication. The moving party did not shift burden. The evidence simply did not show that Plaintiff could not produce evidence to underlying action. The prevailing party is to prepare an order and to comply with CRC 391. (302/REQ/he) Reporter MARIA VISCIO, CSR 12353 (415/515-8070)
